environment_variable_notes.TECH_ROOM_PAGE_ID: This is just the current env var name. Paste the page ID of the main Notion page you want the bridge to use as its default root.
environment_variable_notes.BRIDGE_API_KEY: Make up a long random secret. Use this exact same value in GPT Actions auth.

manual_replacements_before_use: Replace the server URL in NOTION_BRIDGE_GPT_SCHEMA_SHAREABLE.json with your own Netlify site URL.
Paste your real env var values into Netlify before testing.
Save the Action after schema edits, then start a new chat.

common_failures.401_unauthorized: x-bridge-key value mismatch between GPT Actions and Netlify env vars.
common_failures.500_missing_bridge_key: BRIDGE_API_KEY env var is missing or misspelled.
common_failures.500_missing_root_page_id: TECH_ROOM_PAGE_ID env var is missing or misspelled.
common_failures.openapi_parse_error: The schema JSON has a broken comma, quote, or brace.
common_failures.client_response_error: GPT Actions may still be using old auth or an old schema session. Save the Action and start a new chat.

notes: Deploy the whole project folder, not only notion-bridge.js.
Share the target Notion pages and databases with the integration before testing.
list-comments accepts page_id for convenience, but the backend sends block_id to Notion.
This package is usable now and still being refined.
